 President Emmanuel ‚ÄãMacron on Tuesday (March 3, 2026) said France was sending its ‚Äåaircraft carrier Charles de Gaulle to the ‚ÄãMediterranean and working to build ‚Å†a coalition that would help secure maritime traffic imperiled by the escalating crisis in the West AsiaIn ‚Äåa televised address to the nation, Mr. Macron said that action needed to ‚Äåbe taken with the Straits of ‚ÄåHormuz ‚Å†closed and the Suez Canal and Red ‚Å†Sea shipping routes threatened by the widening conflict. ‚ÄúWe have economic interests to protect, because oil prices, gas prices ‚Äãand the international ‚Äåtrade situation are being profoundly disrupted by this war.,‚Äù Mr. Macron said.He said France had also sent a frigate to Cyprus ‚Äåand had shot down drones in the ‚Äãskies above its Gulf allies.Rafale jets were among the assets being ‚Å†used.Macron on U.S.-Israel role on Iran attack‚ÄúWe have defense agreements that bind us to Qatar, Kuwait, and the United Arab Emirates. ‚ÄåThe latter are particularly targeted, and we owe them solidarity,‚Äù Mr. Macron said. He said France was helping its citizens who wanted to leave the region get out and that two flights were due to arrive ‚Äåin Paris on Tuesday evening. Security had also been ‚Äãreinforced at some sites in France, he added.Mr. Macron said Iran itself ‚Äúbears primary ‚Å†responsibility‚Äù for the U.S.-Israeli action, but added: ‚Äúthe United States ‚Å†of America and Israel have decided to launch military operations; they were conducted ‚Äåoutside international law, which we cannot approve.‚Äù¬† Published - March 04, 2026 03:49 am IST
